steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Transitions question. Sentence 1 says the 1920 method "seems rudimentary today." Sentence 2 says the 2015 study confirmed it was "relatively accurate." The blank must signal a concession: despite the apparent crudeness, the method actually worked.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'For example,': signals that what follows illustrates the previous point. Sentence 2 contradicts, not illustrates, sentence 1. ✗
  • Choice B — 'That said,': signals a concession — "despite what was just stated." This correctly bridges the apparent rudimentariness to the evidence of accuracy. ✓
  • Choice C — 'As such,': signals a consequence or result. Sentence 2 is not a result of the method seeming rudimentary. ✗
  • Choice D — 'In other words,': signals a restatement of the same idea. Sentence 2 contradicts sentence 1 rather than restating it. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
